Tonight's amazing results! | Friend --

This is a big night for our campaign: We just won the primaries in Pennsylvania, Maryland, and Delaware! (And more results are on the way!)

Because of you -- and more than 10 million voters across America! -- we will come back to Philadelphia this July for the Democratic National Convention with the most votes and the most pledged delegates, ready to unify our party and win in November.

**[If you’re ready to fight by my side all the way to the White House, chip in right now -- let’s go make history. (And as a thank you, when you make your first donation, we’ll send you a free sticker.)](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=1&ms=MjUzNDMyMzYS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=Nzg0MTI1MDcwS0&mt=1&rt=0)**

There’s much more that unites Democratic voters than divides us. We agree that we have to bring this country together, not pull it further apart. We agree on defending all our rights -- civil rights and voting rights, worker’s rights and women’s rights, LGBT rights and rights for people with disabilities.

And we agree we have to defeat candidates who would threaten those rights, from denying women the right to make our own health care decisions to deporting millions of immigrants to making it harder to vote -- not easier.

If Donald Trump or Ted Cruz gets into the Oval Office, they can reverse all the progress we've made under President Obama with the stroke of a pen.

name: Donna Edwards
subject: Thank you for being a part of this
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Donna Edwards for Senate
body:
Friend,
When I announced my candidacy for the United States Senate, I said I wouldn't run away from who I was or the life I've lived.
I'm a black woman, a single mom who struggled to put food on the table and pay for childcare, a woman who walked into a Planned Parenthood when she needed healthcare, an Air Force brat, and just one of the millions of women who has received unequal pay in the workplace for my equal work as a lawyer, systems engineer, and non-profit executive.
Those experiences shape who I am as a person, my values, and how I legislate in Congress.
History was talked about a lot on this campaign, and it should be -- because history matters.
It matters for the hundreds of young women and girls I met who watched me run for this Senate seat and thought "this is something I can do."
Who will look to the nation's space program, boardrooms, workplaces, and know that they belong.
I think about when I was a young girl and my dad was stationed at Andrews Air Force base. After church on Sundays when the weather was nice we would go to the hill outside of the U.S. Capitol and I would look up at that big white building -- I never dreamed that could be my office.
I hope for all the women and girls across Maryland and the country that I helped shatter that idea -- even just a little bit. Because for every girl who's never told she could run for Congress, run for Senate, be an engineer, or go to law school, I want to say to you right now -- **you can do this.**
Be what you want to be, do what you want to do -- you can be anything you dream of.
This campaign for the U.S. Senate ends tonight, but the values that drive us to fight for what we believe in, and the dreams I hope we inspired in young people to change the face of our democracy, never will.
Thank you for being a part of this with me,
Donna

name: Joe Sestak
subject: Thank you
body:
[](http://action.joesestak.com/page/m/17198ac/3476a20e/da9ca50/274241be/3668785832/VEsH/ "http://joesestak.com")
---

Peter, I write this with a thoughtful soul knowing I can never do justice to people who believed in me by a simple note of "thanks." Your belief in me, tangible in time, effort, and money invested in me, will be the most cherished memory I will keep from my time in politics. And that memory will find a home in the grandest sepulcher of all, the heart of a man enriched by the service of others. I did not reach our journey of U.S. Senate to have been able to return your service by serving America, again. But I am so much richer for having met you in your gift of faith in me. Please know I will remain indebted, always, for your trust that I might have served our country, honorably. Thank you... and warmly, Joe

name: ted@tedcruz.org
subject: The next Vice President of the United States
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY CRUZ FOR PRESIDENT
body:
I wanted you to be the first to know. It's my honor and privilege to introduce you to Carly Fiorina -- the next Vice President of the United States. | [](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/35048009:seNclRC5N:m:1:1320601823:17275098E015370E4F04AA88C9D1ADCD:r)
---
Friend,

I wanted you to be the first to know.

**It's my honor and privilege to introduce you to Carly Fiorina - the next Vice President of the United States.**

Carly and I are about to go on stage to make this important announcement official, but I wanted you - one of my closest and most loyal supporters - to hear it from me first.

As you get to know Carly like I have - I know you will agree that she is the right person and ready to accept this enormous responsibility on behalf of the American people.

| | **Carly is a Leader:** a leader who understands that power never has and never will come from the government - it comes from the people. A leader who knows the principles of the Declaration and the Constitution because she has lived them - a life dedicated to the dignity of work, of our right to pursue our wildest aspirations - and to help others do the same.

**Carly is a Fighter:** a fighter who terrifies Hillary, and she will do the same to our enemies. Carly will fight for the American people with an unbreakable purpose of protecting every one of their rights and ensuring we hand our children and grandchildren a better America than the one before us today.

**Carly has a record of Accomplishment:** a record of both personal and professional accomplishment, Carly was the first woman to lead a Fortune 50 business, and she did it during the worst technology recession in 25 years. Carly saved 80,000 jobs and grew the company to 160,000 jobs.

---|---
With Carly fighting alongside me - I'm more energized than ever to win this nomination, defeat Hillary Clinton, and to reignite liberty in America.

[**Are you with us?**](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/35048009:seNclRC5N:m:1:1320601823:17275098E015370E4F04AA88C9D1ADCD:r)

Today more than ever - at this pivotal moment - I need to know if you will stand with Carly and me to defeat Donald Trump and capture our party's nomination.

What you and I do today will literally determine the course of our nation for years, even decades, to come.

Friend, I am personally asking you for your support today. To win - I must have your financial support.

[ STAND WITH TED AND CARLY ](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/35048009:seNclRC5N:m:1:1320601823:17275098E015370E4F04AA88C9D1ADCD:r)

body:
What Donald said last night about Hillary: | Friend --

After Donald Trump won all five primaries last night, he launched into an absurd diatribe against Hillary: "If Hillary Clinton were a man, I don't think she'd get 5% of the vote ... The only thing she's got going is the woman's card, and the beautiful thing is women don't like her."

You know, after decades in the public eye, Hillary Clinton can take these kinds of attacks.

But this is the kind of dismissal millions of women in America deal with every day. Too many don't get the credit they deserve. Their work isn't respected.

And that brush-off is exactly why women's issues are so often downplayed in our politics -- why women don't get paid equally, struggle without paid family leave, and suffer without access to affordable health care.

Those are the women who are hurt by rhetoric like what we heard last night from Donald Trump, and stand to suffer real damage if his bully pulpit turns into real power to make change.

name: Joe Biden
subject: Game time
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Katie McGinty for Senate
body:
Dear Friend --

Born in Scranton, I know a fair bit about what Pennsylvanians value: Honesty. Grit. Hard work. A fair shot for everyone. **That 's what this state and our country are about. That's what Katie McGinty is about. She embodies those values to her core.** It's who she is.

Daughter of a Philadelphia cop and restaurant hostess. Ninth of ten kids -- who went to St. Joe's as the first in her family to go to a four-year college. A top lawyer who takes on big polluters. A business leader who proves Pennsylvania can create good-paying jobs by protecting our environment. A public servant who understands budgets and laws reflect our values -- investing in public education, providing health care for millions of Pennsylvanians, and protecting the rights of women, workers, and voters -- and the right to be who you are and marry the person you love.

**Friend, it 's game time. Katie just won an extremely competitive primary, and now she's preparing for one of the toughest Senate challenges in the entire country.**

[**Katie needs us by her side. Will you donate $5 or whatever you can today so Katie has the grassroots support to take on her incumbent opponent and win? Defeating Pat Toomey is going to take everything we 've got -- Katie and I are counting on you now.**](https://act.myngp.com/el/2889535369752084480/7956194781447585792?refcode=e20160427.2&refcode2=e20160427.2nd&amount=25)

I served in the Senate for 36 years. I've seen firsthand how important a Democratic Senate majority is in moving the country forward on everything we care about --affordable health care, good-paying middle-class jobs, a great education for our kids. I can go on.

This year, we have a great opportunity to get our majority back -- to end Republican obstruction on the Supreme Court and denial of equal pay for women and protecting Social Security for our seniors.

We need people like Katie who will grab the baton from me and Barack, and keep the progress going to protect everything we've worked so hard to accomplish over eight years on behalf of the country.

**Friend, that 's exactly why it's so important we help Katie out now, early on. We know the other side is going to fight hard to keep this seat.**

[**We need to make sure she has what she needs to win. Will you donate right now so Katie can turn the Senate back to blue?**](https://act.myngp.com/el/2889535369752084480/7956194781447585792?refcode=e20160427.2&refcode2=e20160427.2nd&amount=25)

Thanks for all you do,

Joe Biden
---

name: Beth Hansen, Campaign Manager
subject: RE: The next step
body:
I just wanted to make sure you saw Gov. Kasich's email earlier.

Peter, I just wanted to make
sure you saw Gov. Kasich's email earlier.
We launched a new ad in Oregon
today and with Cruz not campaigning there, we have a great chance
to grab some more delegates.
Oregon is a 100% vote-by-mail
state and they'll receive their ballots this week! Help us get
this ad to more voters!

Thank you for your ongoing
support.
-Beth

--
From: John Kasich
To: Beth Hansen
Date: April 27, 2016
Subject: The next step

Beth - in five states last night,
we finished second and ahead of Cruz in four of them. We also
continued to gain delegates and take another step in stopping
Trump from getting the nomination.
The next step for us is to gain
more delegates in Oregon and many of the voters will cast their
mail-in ballots within the next week!
We have to raise another $65,000
by Friday for our *Oregon Ballot Fund* and ensure that we win
more delegates in Oregon. Can you help us out?

>> CHIP IN $25 <<

>> CHIP IN $50 <<

>> CHIP IN $100 <<

>> CHIP IN $250 <<

We're up to 16 polls in a row now
that show I'm the only candidate who can defeat Hillary Clinton.
Ready to win?
Thank you for all that you're
doing to help the campaign!
John Kasich
Governor of Ohio & Presidential
Candidate

name: Bernie Sanders
subject: Why we fight
body:
#
Sisters and Brothers -
Last night showed again that when we open the political process, change is possible. I am proud that we were able to win a resounding victory in Rhode Island, the one state with an open primary. I want to tell you another reason why our success with independent voters is so important for the future of this country.
For a long time, the political establishment and corporate media in this country have sold a myth that in order to win independent voters, candidates had to appeal to an ideological "center" who wanted Republicans and Democrats to come together to do things like cut social security benefits, pass horrible trade deals, and go to war with Iraq.
But what we are proving through our campaign is that what independents and young people actually want is a departure from the same old establishment ideas. They want transformational change: a $15 minimum wage, health care as a right for all Americans, free college at public universities, and an end to the influence big money on our politics.
Indiana votes in six days. **We have an FEC fundraising deadlinein three days. How we do in each of those will send a powerful message about our willingness to fight for the values we share.** We are fighting for every delegate and every vote until the convention. That's why I have to ask:
[**Can I count on you to contribute $2.70 to our campaign to help us win Indiana, hit our fundraising goals before Saturday 's deadline, and to send a message that you will never stop fighting for the values we share?**](https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/a-progressive-agenda?refcode=em160427-v1-sub-a "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/a-progressive-agenda?refcode=em160427-v1-sub-a")
Democrats should recognize that the ticket with the best chance of winning this November must attract support from independents as well as Democrats. But just as importantly, they should recognize that the progressive vision we share is the best way to win this election and elections for generations to come.
In solidarity,
Bernie Sanders
**[Contribute](https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/a-progressive-agenda?refcode=em160427-v1-sub-a "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/a-progressive-agenda?refcode=em160427-v1-sub-a")**

name: Chris Van Hollen
subject: my deepest thanks
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate
body:

>
> Friend,
>
> We did it.
>
> Yesterday Democrats from across our great state voted decisively for a vision of working together to move Maryland and our country forward.
>
> **This victory belongs to each of you. Without a massive team of volunteers and supporters like you, friend, this would not have been possible.**
>
> I'm proud of what we have accomplished together and look forward to getting our message out to every voter in every part of Maryland between now and the November general election.
>
> We are building a grassroots movement that can be a powerful force for change as part of a new Democratic majority in the U.S. Senate. I look forward to continuing our work together.
>
> Thank you for being a part of this journey,
>
> Chris
